title: Topo public module and feature inventory
planningSource: The active hmem Topo 1.0 project tree and the package.yaml exposed-modules lists are the sources of truth for this inventory.

packages.name: topo
packages.role: Stable terrain, simulation, persistence, overlay, plugin-contract, and command DTO library.

packages.name: topo-seer
packages.role: SDL application host that will own AppService, UI, HTTP/OpenAPI, and plugin supervision.

packages.name: topo-plugin-sdk
packages.role: Canonical Haskell SDK for plugin manifests, payload helpers, and runtime connection behavior.

featureGroups.title: Core model, coordinates, metadata, configuration, units, and deterministic helpers
featureGroups.uiSurface: Topo-seer state, terrain inspector, config panels, and unit display; M9 expands field-level inspector coverage.
featureGroups.serviceSurface: Planned AppService state/config/terrain services in M2; existing command DTOs are compatibility inputs.
featureGroups.httpSurface: Planned M3 routes for state, config, version, terrain hex/chunk queries, units/metadata, and OpenAPI schemas.
planRefs: M9 terrain inspector and feature matrix

featureGroups.title: Pipeline, world generation, tectonics, terrain forms, erosion, and volcanism
featureGroups.uiSurface: Topo-seer generation controls and existing view modes; M9 adds pipeline diagnostics and inspector sections for terrain, tectonics, erosion, hypsometry, terrain forms, and volcanism.
featureGroups.serviceSurface: Planned AppService generation, terrain, pipeline-stage, dependency-closure, and diagnostics services.
featureGroups.httpSurface: Planned M3 routes for generation/status, terrain queries, pipeline stages, parameters, diagnostics, and export metadata.

featureGroups.title: Hydrology, rivers, water bodies, water table, glaciers, and ocean currents
featureGroups.uiSurface: Existing river/water-related rendering and overlays; M9 adds terrain inspector sections and view-mode metadata for water, ice, and currents.
featureGroups.serviceSurface: Planned AppService terrain inspector and hydrology query services.
featureGroups.httpSurface: Planned terrain hex/chunk and feature metadata routes covering rivers, water bodies, water table, glaciers, and currents.

featureGroups.title: Climate, weather, biome refinement, soil, and vegetation
featureGroups.uiSurface: Existing climate/weather/biome view modes and config tabs; M9 adds inspector sections, legends, units, and weather timeline/status coverage.
featureGroups.serviceSurface: Planned AppService climate/weather/biome/soil/vegetation query and metadata services.
featureGroups.httpSurface: Planned terrain hex/chunk routes and feature metadata for climate, weather, biomes, soil, and vegetation.
planRefs: M9 view mode registry, legends, and export mappings

featureGroups.title: Overlays, schema/provenance/indexing, storage, world bundles, mesh, and export
featureGroups.uiSurface: Existing overlay selection/rendering and persistence actions; M9 adds overlay manager, schema/provenance inspector, import/export dialogs, and copy-as-JSON affordances.
featureGroups.serviceSurface: Planned AppService overlay/schema/provenance, persistence, import/export, mesh/sample, and diagnostics services.
featureGroups.httpSurface: Planned overlay, schema, provenance, world save/load/list, export, and terrain sample routes.
planRefs: M9 overlay manager and export/import dialogs

featureGroups.title: Simulation and simulation DAG
featureGroups.uiSurface: Existing topo-seer simulation controls; M9 adds DAG/status/tick logs and plugin simulation-node visibility.
featureGroups.serviceSurface: Planned AppService simulation tick, state, DAG status, and diagnostics services.
featureGroups.httpSurface: Planned simulation state, tick, DAG, and event routes.
planRefs: M9 simulation DAG and plugin data-source/resource feature surfaces

featureGroups.title: Plugin capability, manifest, RPC, transport, payload, and data-resource contracts
featureGroups.uiSurface: Existing plugin manager status surfaces are partial; M5-M7 and M9 add lifecycle, resource, dependency, and backend-neutral external data-source status UI.
featureGroups.serviceSurface: Planned AppService plugin status, dependency, resource, CRUD, lifecycle, and external data-source declaration/status services.
featureGroups.httpSurface: Planned plugin status, params, dependencies, resources, external data-source status, schema, and CRUD routes.
planRefs: M5 plugin supervisor and production transport
M6 manifest v3 and CRUD hardening

featureGroups.title: Command DTO compatibility types
featureGroups.uiSurface: Indirectly used by topo-seer internal/test command compatibility paths; not a standalone user-facing UI surface.
featureGroups.serviceSurface: M2 converts command handling into typed AppService operations while preserving command DTO compatibility where needed.
featureGroups.httpSurface: No direct public 1.0 automation surface; DTOs should be replaced by or mapped to topo-api route DTOs.

featureGroups.title: Topo-seer actor runtime, terrain/cache workers, plugin manager, logging, snapshots, and UI state actor
featureGroups.uiSurface: Runtime-backed status appears in the SDL UI; M2 and M8 reduce direct UI coupling by routing mutations through AppService and testable reducers.
featureGroups.serviceSurface: Planned AppService adapters over actors for app state, terrain/cache work, logs, plugins, simulation, snapshots, and async status.
featureGroups.httpSurface: Planned state, events, logs, plugin status, simulation status, and generation/terrain status routes.

featureGroups.title: Command IPC, dispatch, and UI action compatibility adapters
featureGroups.uiSurface: Current UI and internal/test command IPC compatibility paths use command/UI-action adapters; M2 moves behavior behind AppService.
featureGroups.serviceSurface: Compatibility adapter over AppService after M2; current command dispatch is the seed inventory for service operations.
featureGroups.httpSurface: No direct public 1.0 automation surface; M3 maps public operations to HTTP/OpenAPI routes and M4 removes MCP dependence. Legacy command IPC has no public 1.0 exception.

featureGroups.title: Application boot, runtime config, snapshots, persistence, screenshots, system wiring, and timing
featureGroups.uiSurface: Existing app shell, config state, screenshot, save/load, and runtime actions; M8 splits Seer.System and M9 adds persistence/export/import diagnostics.
featureGroups.serviceSurface: Planned AppService app/config/world persistence, screenshot, runtime status, and diagnostics services.

featureGroups.title: SDL UI drawing, input routing, editor, renderer, widgets, layout, theme, terrain atlas/cache, and view rendering
featureGroups.uiSurface: Primary SDL UI surface; M8 componentizes panels/reducers/layout/draw commands and M9 closes feature-surface gaps.
featureGroups.serviceSurface: M2/M8 route mutating UI actions through AppService; read models become testable view models where practical.
featureGroups.httpSurface: Public HTTP should expose state/metadata/actions rather than SDL rendering internals; routes are planned for UI state, panels, view modes, terrain inspector, editor, overlays, and screenshots.

featureGroups.title: Plugin SDK declarations, payload helpers, terrain/overlay helpers, and runner
featureGroups.uiSurface: Developer-facing SDK, not a runtime UI surface; plugin-generated declarations must surface through plugin status/resource UI after M5-M9.
featureGroups.serviceSurface: SDK declarations feed host manifest/protocol/resource services; M6 makes SDK-generated manifest v3 canonical.
featureGroups.httpSurface: No direct HTTP endpoints for SDK modules; generated plugin declarations appear through plugin/status/resource/data routes.
